116145.explanation: We prioritize the quality of a leader. The gender of that person is secondary. At the same time, we pursue equal representation in the executive board. When searching for a new mayor, it is a plus for HOI Werkt if the candidate is a woman.

116146.explanation: A referendum increases involvement, support, and transparency, but political issues are more complex than 'agree' or 'disagree.' HOI Werkt therefore considers for each topic how best to involve residents, for example through citizen assemblies. This way, residents get a voice early in the process.

116147.explanation: If residents can perform certain municipal tasks better than the municipality or a market party, we embrace this idea. We already have good experiences with the right to challenge in our municipality. It increases resident involvement, lowers costs, and results in plans that fit the local situation.

116150.explanation: We support local initiatives for generating sustainable energy and sustainability efforts, such as Sustainable Biest-Houtakker. Support can be financial but also consist of knowledge or deployment of civil servant hours. Local initiatives advance the energy transition.

116152.explanation: As a municipality, we have a legal obligation from the national government to provide long-term shelter for 106 asylum seekers. We will arrange this, but not with a large asylum seekers' center. HOI Werkt aims for small-scale shelter to keep the impact on the surroundings limited.

116154.explanation: There are several periods each year when wood stoves and open fireplaces cause health problems. Under weather conditions with little wind, fine dust emissions cause asthma complaints among nearby residents. Therefore, burning at those times should be discouraged as much as possible.

116156.explanation: The municipality is not the primary organization to initiate fireworks shows. Our residents and entrepreneurs can do this much better. If they show there is demand and take the initiative to organize something, we have a positive attitude toward this.

116160.explanation: We preserve the countryside for nature, recreation, agriculture, and suitable businesses on vacated agricultural sites. We build houses as much as possible inside or adjacent to the villages near facilities. Farmers can diversify their income with recreation and local sales of their products.

116163.explanation: In Hilvarenbeek, we have a subsidy so that all children can be introduced to music and culture. It is good for development and one of the reasons our municipality has a rich association life and many local events.

116166.explanation: Especially in the countryside, challenges exist such as drug dumping and illegal motocross riders. We do not think more municipal enforcement officers are the solution. We prefer to collaborate with neighboring municipalities and rely on the eyes and ears of our residents.

116167.explanation: Camera surveillance must always serve a purpose, for example in areas with high crime. There are already cameras in various places in our municipality. Currently, HOI Werkt sees no need to expand camera surveillance in our municipality.

116170.explanation: Our municipality has a special need for housing for two target groups: starters and seniors. By building suitable homes for seniors, the housing market gains momentum. They often leave behind large family homes where a starting family can live.

116171.explanation: Holiday parks are often not designed to function as residential neighborhoods. They are located outside village centers and far from facilities. The holiday homes themselves often do not meet building standards. This can be a temporary solution, but long term, we focus on building within the centers.

116175.explanation: Raising the income limit only has a temporary effect. A better approach is tailored solutions for debts, for example. Furthermore, we must focus on getting people into paid work, as we do through Diamantgroep.

116177.explanation: Currently, there are about a hundred (semi-)public charging points in the municipality for 380 electric cars. Thanks to good charging infrastructure, people choose electric cars faster. The municipality should continue investing in this.

116179.explanation: Care is organized regionally. The aim is good care and support for residents who can no longer care for themselves or cannot find care within their own network. They have freedom of choice among various procured care providers.

116182.explanation: Hilvarenbeek has a beautiful tourism sector. We believe tourists should make a fair contribution. The tourist tax must be distributed more fairly. A small campsite pays the full rate, while De Beekse Bergen pays less thanks to a local tax deal. That must change.

116183.explanation: In Hilvarenbeek, there is space for businesses to grow. We see opportunities by better utilizing existing sites, spatially completing areas, or using former farm locations as small-scale business sites. New industrial estates are our last option.

116184.explanation: Retailers must have the space they need. If they want to open on Sundays, HOI Werkt believes they should be allowed to do so.

116185.explanation: Currently, 15% of vacated social rental homes are reserved for urgent seekers, including status holders. It is important that status holders can participate. This requires a place in society. Therefore, HOI Werkt supports reserving a small portion of the homes.

116186.explanation: Processing the waste delivered at the recycling center costs the municipality money. In our view, those who produce waste should also bear the costs. We do consider whether certain waste streams (for example, green waste) can be delivered free of charge.

116187.explanation: Some time ago, four solar parks covering a total of 50 hectares were permitted. This is a big step for sustainably generating the electricity we need. At this time, that is sufficient, and we will not allow additional large-scale solar parks.

116190.explanation: Brabants dialect is a valuable asset but not part of education. Children have a full program for basic skills such as language, arithmetic, and physical education. Schools know best how to schedule this time. You learn Brabants mainly by participating in our society.

116195.explanation: At most times, there are sufficient parking spaces available in the center, but they are not always easy for everyone to find. Therefore, we must better guide motorists to available parking places. This is more effective than creating new parking spaces.

116197.explanation: In recent years, HOI Werkt has called for a good balance between health and agricultural development in the countryside. That balance is currently lost. We aim for a chemical-free countryside by 2040 and are working towards chemical-free zones around the village centers.

116199.explanation: In recent years, we have tried to set up several temporary housing projects. There appears to be insufficient demand, so we choose other housing solutions.

116145.explanation: According to Hilvarenbeek’s VVD, the quality of aldermen is paramount.

116146.explanation: Residents elect the municipal council to make choices. In exceptional cases, a referendum may be conceivable.

116147.explanation: This can be a good choice within the context of the right to challenge. Sometimes, however, this is not possible or wise. The public interest must be leading in this.

116150.explanation: We help homeowners to become more sustainable, for example through the sustainability loan.

116152.explanation: The municipality of Hilvarenbeek already does a lot for asylum reception. Therefore, we currently see no expansion of reception locations.

116154.explanation: We focus on educating people about wood burning. A ban goes too far for us.

116156.explanation: An initiative for a fireworks show should come from the community. The municipality can possibly support this.

116160.explanation: There may be room for the development of additional homes on agricultural estates, provided they fit the scale and area where the business is located. In addition, we see more possibilities for parcel splitting, for example at VAB locations, where this is spatially and functionally acceptable.

116163.explanation: The municipality already facilitates this through the backpack scheme.

116166.explanation: We want sufficient enforcement officers (BOAs) in our own municipality to ensure enforcement. Currently, we share BOAs with Oisterwijk and Goirle. We no longer want this. With the funds freed up, we will pay our own BOAs.

116167.explanation: Camera surveillance is not an end in itself, but if it is useful and necessary for safety in our municipality, we support it.

116170.explanation: Many seniors would like to move to a better-suited home. Respond to that by building life-course resistant and zero-step homes that seniors need. This promotes flow, which also creates space for starters again.

116171.explanation: The municipality must carefully weigh whether this is wise and look at it per park. And certainly not apply it generally.

116175.explanation: We want to maintain the existing arrangements and limits for poverty support.

116177.explanation: We support steady expansion of charging points for electric cars. The expansion must be demand-driven.

116179.explanation: Freedom of choice is important, but not unlimited. It depends on the social interest and any additional costs involved. When there is a clear disproportion between costs and benefits, that choice is not self-evident.

116182.explanation: We do not increase taxes such as property tax (OZB) and levies, except with the inflation index. The only exception is if it is known in advance that the proceeds go to a specifically named purpose with great social importance, and if there is no other coverage.

116183.explanation: We strongly support sufficient business parks and the retention of existing sites. To keep our local economy vibrant, we want more space for growing and new SMEs. Currently, there are far too few possibilities.

116184.explanation: The municipality already allows stores to be open all year round on Sundays. As a result, every Sunday can already be a shopping Sunday and no additional shopping Sundays can be added.

116185.explanation: No priority for status holders when allocating rental homes.

116186.explanation: We want to maintain the current regulation: some waste streams are free, for other waste streams you have to pay.

116187.explanation: We are strongly committed to solar panels on roofs. We do this on municipal properties, but also on homes and (agricultural) businesses. New solar parks on, for example, agricultural land are no longer an option.

116190.explanation: Paying attention to Brabantian dialect at schools is not a task of the municipality. This is the responsibility of the schools themselves and/or the parents/guardians.

116195.explanation: There is high parking pressure in the center of Hilvarenbeek, but creating new parking spaces strongly depends on the opportunities that arise.

116197.explanation: We want to provide tailored solutions, because targeted measures (such as drift-reducing techniques and green hedges) can find solutions that do justice to all interests: those of the home seekers, the health of our residents, and the business operations of agricultural entrepreneurs.

116199.explanation: Temporary housing is fine under conditions, but not too many together and with good communication with the surroundings. In any case, we do not support the plans the council presented in 2025. It would be better to shorten project lead times in general.

116147.explanation: Management of parks and association buildings is fine, but a sports hall/pool requires professional expertise.

116150.explanation: We want to facilitate and offer subsidies that are available.

116152.explanation: We have a small shelter for refugees in Baarschot, which will close. A small-scale shelter must return for this.

116154.explanation: Wood burning for private use can continue, with encouragement to limit it during bad weather conditions.

116156.explanation: The municipality must stimulate and facilitate together with hospitality and entrepreneurs.

116160.explanation: Within the framework of the environmental vision, we see opportunities to realize residential clusters in the rural area.

116163.explanation: The backpack for music and other artistic activities should only be maintained for local minimum income households.

116166.explanation: Together with Goirle and Oisterwijk, we maintain the staffing levels.

116167.explanation: Fortunately, we still have a society where this is not necessary.

116170.explanation: We explicitly encourage clustered housing units where seniors can help each other.

116171.explanation: Holiday homes are for tourists. We would like to build many more so that this is not necessary.

116175.explanation: We want tailored solutions where needed.

116177.explanation: If electric cars expand, then this must also be facilitated.

116179.explanation: We will strive to take this into account as much as possible.

116182.explanation: All tourist facilities should be treated equally, including Beekse Bergen.

116184.explanation: We prefer to leave that to the entrepreneurs.

116185.explanation: Every resident is equal, including status holders living in Hilvarenbeek.

116186.explanation: Yes, we want to encourage proper waste separation into raw materials.

116187.explanation: Expansion on poor agricultural land should remain possible.

116190.explanation: The Brabant cultures can already be addressed in schools.

116195.explanation: Parking for shopping is not feasible.

116197.explanation: Farmers are encouraged to be more mindful when spraying crop protection products near residences.

116199.explanation: Temporary housing can be used if rapid expansion of regular housing is not possible.

statements.title: Temporary housing should be provided in the municipality of Hilvarenbeek.
In Hilvarenbeek the use of fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Hilvarenbeek moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Hilvarenbeek should employ more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The residents of Hilvarenbeek should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Hilvarenbeek moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
More business parks should be developed in Hilvarenbeek.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
There should be more camera surveillance in Hilvarenbeek.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.
The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: More small rental and owner-occupied homes must be built in Hilvarenbeek for older people.
The municipality should continue to reserve a fixed percentage of rental homes for refugees with a residence permit.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet een vast percentage van de huurwoningen blijven reserveren voor vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
There should be more parking spaces in the centre of Hilvarenbeek.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Brabant dialect.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should ban the use of agricultural pesticides near homes.
